meta_description: Create clear word-order diagrams for translation, language learning, and linguistics teaching.
meta_keywords: word order, linguistics, translation, language learning, syntax visualization
meta_image_alt: Preview of the Word Order Illustrator with linked words across two languages.
input_guidance: Words are split automatically with locale-aware segmentation when available, or by spaces and punctuation as a fallback. Use {delimiter} for finer control, e.g. {example}
input_gloss_empty: Add words first to enter glosses.
footer_info: This application does not claim rights over the illustrations you create here. How you use or share them is completely up to you. Sharing this tool is appreciated.
confirm_delete_sentence: Are you sure you want to delete this sentence?
confirm_new: Are you sure you want to create a new illustration in this tab? Sentences and connections will be cleared.
confirm_close_tab: Close "{name}"? Sentences and connections in this tab will be discarded.
help_tagline: A bitext word-alignment and word-order illustrator.
help_intro: Paste sentences in any languages, then connect matching words across rows. The tool draws curved connectors so reordered translations, one-to-many mappings, and many-to-one mappings all stay readable.
help_how_step1: Type or paste a sentence into the input box and press Add. Words are split automatically with locale-aware segmentation.
help_how_step2: Click a word in one row, then a matching word in an adjacent row, then press Confirm to connect them. Click an already-linked word to edit the group.
help_how_step3: Use the pencil icon to fine-tune tokenization (merge or split tokens), and optionally fill in an interlinear gloss row.
help_how_step4: Adjust spacing, curvature, fonts, and colors in the Options panel. Export as SVG or PNG when you're done.
help_feature_tokenization: Locale-aware tokenization for CJK, Thai, and more — no manual split characters needed.
help_feature_gloss: Interlinear gloss row above each sentence for Leipzig-style annotations.
help_feature_ruby: Ruby annotation support for furigana (Japanese), zhuyin/pinyin (Chinese), and similar.
help_feature_tabs: Work on multiple illustrations side by side using tabs.
help_feature_autosave: Everything autosaves locally to your browser — refreshing or closing the tab won't lose work.
help_feature_export: Export to SVG (vector) or PNG (raster), or save your project as JSON to share or back up.
